name: Suwar ibn Abi Humayr
title: The Wounded Captive
shortDescription: A warrior who was severely wounded and captured on Ashura, but attained martyrdom after enduring six months of pain from his wounds.
fullStory:
Sawwar ibn Abi Umayr (or Humayr) al-Nahmi was a brave Shia and fighter from Kufa. Understanding the critical situation, he reached Karbala before the roads were completely closed to wield his sword alongside Imam Hussain (AS).

On the day of Ashura, Sawwar entered the battlefield with unparalleled bravery. An experienced warrior, he engaged in a fierce battle with Umar Sa'd's soldiers and inflicted many casualties upon them.

During this unequal combat, Sawwar was targeted by multiple sword and spear strikes and fell half-dead to the ground due to severe injuries. The Kufans, enraged by his fighting, intended to behead him, but a group of his relatives in the Kufan army intervened and took him captive.

Sawwar was taken to Ibn Ziyad in Kufa. Ibn Ziyad intended to kill him, but through the intercession of his tribe, he changed his mind and imprisoned him with a body full of wounds. Sawwar endured the pain and suffering from his Karbala wounds for six months.

Finally, this devoted warrior, after six months of resistance, attained martyrdom due to those same wounds inflicted on Ashura, and his pure soul joined his martyred companions.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى الجَريحِ المَأْسُورِ، سَوّارِ بْنِ أَبي حِمْيَرٍ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon the wounded captive, Sawwar ibn Abi Humayr.
